title: Layer envelope — minimal stable core for any Atlas-registered layer
description: ONE envelope shape for both staging and normalized layers. Discriminator is the 'stage' field. Per PIPELINE_FLOW.md §'Minimal stable core': dataset_id, layer_id, layer_type, schema_version, coordinate scope, sample scope, source files, provenance, status. The 'payload' field is loose for staging (any JSON) and validated against <atlas>/registries/schemas/schema_out/<schema_version>.schema.json for normalized. Atlases may extend with extra top-level fields — additionalProperties is true. Reversible: a staging layer can later be normalized into one (or many) normalized layers; provenance.source_layer_ids preserves the lineage.

layer_id.description: Stable id, globally unique within the atlas registry.

schema_version.description: Name of the schema this payload validates against, e.g. 'fst_windows_v1' (normalized) or 'staging_inversion_candidate_v0' (staging). Lives in <atlas>/registries/schemas/schema_out/<schema_version>.schema.json.

stage.description: staging = loose capture, payload not strictly validated; normalized = Atlas-renderable, payload validated against schema_version.

coordinate.description: Optional coordinate scope. Null/absent = genome-wide or non-coordinate (e.g. sample metadata).

source_files.description: Paths to the raw files this layer was extracted from. Never lose this — it's the link back to the upstream pipeline.

source_layer_ids.description: FKs into the registry — staging or other normalized layers this was derived from. Lets a converter trace back to the staging capture.

config_hash.description: sha256 of the action params. Lets integrity checks detect param drift.

status.description: Lifecycle. 'review' = new/unaudited; 'active' = serve to Atlas; 'stale' = integrity check flagged drift; 'superseded' = a newer normalized layer covers this; 'deprecated' = hide.

payload.description: The typed data the Atlas renders. For stage='normalized' the dispatcher validates this against schema_out/<schema_version>.schema.json. For stage='staging' the payload is any JSON (loose capture).
